metadata.curriculum: Cambridge International AS & A Level Biology (9700)
metadata.description: A-Level Biology topics with prerequisite ordering, colored by component
sources: Cambridge International AS & A Level Biology 9700 Syllabus

nodes.label: Microscopy & Cell Measurements
nodes.description: Light and electron microscopes, magnification, resolution, calculating actual size of specimens

nodes.label: Eukaryotic Cell Structure
nodes.description: Ultrastructure of eukaryotic cells: nucleus, mitochondria, endoplasmic reticulum, Golgi apparatus, lysosomes, ribosomes, centrioles, chloroplasts, vacuoles

nodes.label: Prokaryotic Cell Structure
nodes.description: Structure of prokaryotic cells: cell wall, capsule, plasmids, nucleoid, flagella; comparison with eukaryotic cells

nodes.label: Viruses
nodes.description: Structure of viruses as non-cellular, acellular agents; comparison with cells; examples including bacteriophages and HIV

nodes.label: Water & Inorganic Ions
nodes.description: Properties of water: hydrogen bonding, cohesion, solvent properties, thermal properties; roles of inorganic ions in biology

nodes.label: Carbohydrates
nodes.description: Monosaccharides, disaccharides and polysaccharides; glycosidic bonds; structure and function of glucose, sucrose, starch, glycogen and cellulose

nodes.label: Lipids
nodes.description: Triglycerides and phospholipids; ester bonds; saturated and unsaturated fatty acids; roles of lipids in energy storage, insulation and membranes

nodes.label: Proteins
nodes.description: Amino acids and peptide bonds; primary, secondary, tertiary and quaternary structure; fibrous and globular proteins; haemoglobin, collagen

nodes.label: Nucleic Acids (AS Introduction)
nodes.description: Structure of nucleotides, DNA and RNA; sugar-phosphate backbone; complementary base pairing; differences between DNA and RNA

nodes.label: Biochemical Tests
nodes.description: Benedict's test for reducing sugars, iodine test for starch, emulsion test for lipids, biuret test for proteins; use of chromatography

nodes.label: Enzyme Mode of Action
nodes.description: Lock-and-key and induced-fit hypotheses; activation energy; enzyme-substrate complex; specificity

nodes.label: Factors Affecting Enzyme Activity
nodes.description: Effects of temperature, pH, enzyme concentration and substrate concentration on rate of reaction; Vmax and Km

nodes.label: Enzyme Inhibition
nodes.description: Competitive and non-competitive inhibition; reversible and irreversible inhibitors; end-product inhibition and metabolic pathways

nodes.label: Immobilised Enzymes
nodes.description: Methods of immobilising enzymes; advantages of immobilised enzymes in biotechnology and industry

nodes.label: Cell Membrane Structure
nodes.description: Fluid mosaic model; phospholipid bilayer; roles of cholesterol, glycoproteins, glycolipids and channel/carrier proteins

nodes.label: Passive Transport
nodes.description: Diffusion, facilitated diffusion and osmosis; water potential; effects on plant and animal cells; investigation of osmosis

nodes.label: Active Transport & Bulk Transport
nodes.description: Active transport using carrier proteins and ATP; endocytosis (phagocytosis, pinocytosis) and exocytosis

nodes.label: Cell Signalling
nodes.description: Cell-to-cell communication; receptor proteins on cell surface membranes; roles of hormones and neurotransmitters as ligands

nodes.label: The Cell Cycle & Interphase
nodes.description: Stages of the cell cycle: G1, S phase (DNA replication), G2 and M phase; checkpoints and control of the cell cycle

nodes.label: Mitosis
nodes.description: Stages of mitosis: prophase, metaphase, anaphase, telophase; cytokinesis; role of mitosis in growth, repair and asexual reproduction

nodes.label: Cancer & Uncontrolled Cell Division
nodes.description: Uncontrolled mitosis; tumour formation; role of oncogenes and tumour suppressor genes; mutagens and carcinogens

nodes.label: DNA Replication
nodes.description: Semi-conservative replication; role of DNA helicase, DNA polymerase; Meselson-Stahl experiment; leading and lagging strands

nodes.label: Transcription
nodes.description: Transcription of DNA to mRNA; role of RNA polymerase; template and coding strands; processing of mRNA in eukaryotes

nodes.label: Translation
nodes.description: Translation at ribosomes; role of mRNA, tRNA and rRNA; codons and anticodons; the genetic code; polypeptide assembly

nodes.label: Gene Mutations
nodes.description: Types of gene mutation: substitution, insertion, deletion; frameshift mutations; effect on protein structure; sickle cell anaemia

nodes.label: Xylem & Water Transport
nodes.description: Structure of xylem vessels; cohesion-tension theory; transpiration pull; adhesion; root pressure

nodes.label: Transpiration
nodes.description: Transpiration and factors affecting rate; stomata and guard cells; xerophytes and hydrophytes; use of potometers

nodes.label: Phloem & Translocation
nodes.description: Structure of phloem sieve tubes and companion cells; translocation; mass flow hypothesis; sources and sinks

nodes.label: Mineral Ion Uptake in Plants
nodes.description: Absorption of mineral ions by root hair cells; role of active transport; symplast and apoplast pathways; Casparian strip

nodes.label: Heart Structure & Function
nodes.description: Structure of the mammalian heart; cardiac cycle; atrial and ventricular systole/diastole; heart sounds; pressure and volume changes

nodes.label: Blood Vessels
nodes.description: Structure and function of arteries, arterioles, capillaries, venules and veins; relationship between structure and function

nodes.label: Blood & Haemoglobin
nodes.description: Composition of blood; red blood cells; oxygen dissociation curves; Bohr effect; foetal haemoglobin; transport of CO2

nodes.label: Double Circulatory System
nodes.description: Double circulation in mammals; pulmonary and systemic circuits; advantages of double circulation; comparison with single circulation in fish

nodes.label: Cardiovascular Disease
nodes.description: Atherosclerosis; coronary heart disease; risk factors including diet, smoking and genetics; treatment with stents and statins

nodes.label: Gas Exchange Surfaces
nodes.description: Features of efficient gas exchange surfaces; large surface area, thin barrier, ventilation, blood supply; Fick's law

nodes.label: Gas Exchange in the Lungs
nodes.description: Structure of the mammalian lungs; trachea, bronchi, bronchioles, alveoli; ventilation mechanism; diaphragm and intercostal muscles

nodes.label: Smoking & Lung Disease
nodes.description: Effects of tar, nicotine and carbon monoxide; chronic bronchitis, emphysema and lung cancer; evidence linking smoking to disease

nodes.label: Pathogens & Transmission
nodes.description: Types of pathogen: bacteria, viruses, fungi, protoctists; modes of transmission: direct, indirect, vectors; examples including cholera, malaria, TB, HIV/AIDS

nodes.label: Cholera
nodes.description: Vibrio cholerae; transmission via contaminated water; mechanism of action of cholera toxin; effect on ion transport and water loss; oral rehydration therapy

nodes.label: Malaria
nodes.description: Plasmodium life cycle; transmission by Anopheles mosquito; symptoms; control strategies including insecticides, bed nets and antimalarial drugs

nodes.label: Tuberculosis (TB)
nodes.description: Mycobacterium tuberculosis; transmission by droplet infection; primary and secondary TB; antibiotic resistance; BCG vaccination

nodes.label: HIV/AIDS
nodes.description: Structure of HIV; mode of transmission; replication in T helper cells; progression to AIDS; antiretroviral therapy

nodes.label: Antibiotics & Antibiotic Resistance
nodes.description: Mechanism of action of antibiotics; antibiotic resistance and natural selection; MRSA; strategies to reduce resistance

nodes.label: Non-Specific Defence Mechanisms
nodes.description: Barriers to infection: skin, mucous membranes, stomach acid; phagocytosis by neutrophils and macrophages; inflammation

nodes.label: Specific Immune Response
nodes.description: Antigens; T lymphocytes (helper, killer, memory); B lymphocytes; clonal selection and expansion; plasma cells and antibody production

nodes.label: Antibodies
nodes.description: Structure of antibodies; variable and constant regions; antigen-antibody specificity; agglutination, neutralisation and opsonisation

nodes.label: Vaccination
nodes.description: Active and passive immunity; natural and artificial immunity; principles of vaccination; herd immunity; vaccine development

nodes.label: Monoclonal Antibodies
nodes.description: Production of monoclonal antibodies using hybridoma cells; uses in diagnosis (ELISA, pregnancy tests) and treatment

nodes.label: ATP & Energy
nodes.description: Structure of ATP; role as universal energy currency; ATP hydrolysis and synthesis; coupling of reactions

nodes.label: Glycolysis
nodes.description: Glycolysis in the cytoplasm; phosphorylation of glucose; production of pyruvate, ATP and reduced NAD; substrate-level phosphorylation

nodes.label: Link Reaction
nodes.description: Oxidative decarboxylation of pyruvate; formation of acetyl CoA; production of CO2 and reduced NAD; location in mitochondrial matrix

nodes.label: Krebs Cycle
nodes.description: Krebs cycle in the mitochondrial matrix; decarboxylation and dehydrogenation; production of reduced NAD, reduced FAD, ATP and CO2

nodes.label: Oxidative Phosphorylation
nodes.description: Electron transport chain on inner mitochondrial membrane; chemiosmosis; role of oxygen as final electron acceptor; ATP synthase; total ATP yield

nodes.label: Anaerobic Respiration
nodes.description: Anaerobic pathways in animals (lactate) and plants/yeast (ethanol); oxygen debt; comparison of aerobic and anaerobic ATP yield

nodes.label: Respiratory Substrates
nodes.description: Use of lipids and amino acids as respiratory substrates; respiratory quotient (RQ); use of respirometers

nodes.label: Chloroplast Structure & Pigments
nodes.description: Structure of chloroplasts; thylakoids and grana; stroma; photosynthetic pigments: chlorophyll a, b, carotenoids; absorption and action spectra

nodes.label: Light-Dependent Reactions
nodes.description: Photophosphorylation (cyclic and non-cyclic); photolysis of water; electron transport chain in thylakoid membranes; production of ATP and reduced NADP

nodes.label: Calvin Cycle
nodes.description: Carbon fixation by RuBisCO; reduction of GP to TP using ATP and reduced NADP; regeneration of RuBP; production of organic molecules

nodes.label: Limiting Factors of Photosynthesis
nodes.description: Effects of light intensity, CO2 concentration and temperature on rate of photosynthesis; interactions between limiting factors; glasshouse management

nodes.label: C4 & CAM Plants
nodes.description: C4 carbon fixation; role of PEP carboxylase; bundle sheath cells; CAM plants; adaptations to hot, dry environments

nodes.label: Principles of Homeostasis
nodes.description: Negative feedback; positive feedback; sensory receptors, coordination centres and effectors; importance of maintaining stable internal conditions

nodes.label: Thermoregulation
nodes.description: Role of the hypothalamus; vasodilation and vasoconstriction; sweating; shivering; role of skin; ectotherms and endotherms

nodes.label: Blood Glucose Regulation
nodes.description: Roles of insulin and glucagon; islets of Langerhans (alpha and beta cells); glycogenesis, glycogenolysis, gluconeogenesis; diabetes types 1 and 2

nodes.label: Kidney Structure & Ultrafiltration
nodes.description: Structure of the nephron; ultrafiltration in the Bowman's capsule; role of podocytes and basement membrane; glomerular filtrate

nodes.label: Selective Reabsorption & Osmoregulation
nodes.description: Selective reabsorption in the proximal convoluted tubule; loop of Henle and countercurrent multiplier; ADH and water reabsorption; osmoregulation

nodes.label: Kidney Failure & Treatment
nodes.description: Causes of kidney failure; haemodialysis; peritoneal dialysis; kidney transplant; comparison of treatments

nodes.label: Nervous System Overview
nodes.description: Central and peripheral nervous system; sensory, relay and motor neurones; reflex arcs; voluntary and involuntary actions

nodes.label: Nerve Impulses
nodes.description: Resting potential and action potential; depolarisation and repolarisation; role of sodium and potassium ion channels; saltatory conduction; myelination

nodes.label: Synapses
nodes.description: Structure of a cholinergic synapse; role of neurotransmitters (acetylcholine); synaptic transmission; excitatory and inhibitory synapses; summation

nodes.label: Sensory Receptors
nodes.description: Pacinian corpuscle as a receptor; generator potential and threshold; structure and function of the retina; rods and cones; role of rhodopsin

nodes.label: Muscle Contraction
nodes.description: Structure of skeletal muscle; sarcomere; sliding filament theory; roles of actin, myosin, troponin, tropomyosin and calcium ions; neuromuscular junction

nodes.label: Hormonal Communication
nodes.description: Endocrine glands and hormones; first and second messengers; adrenaline and its effects; comparison of nervous and hormonal communication

nodes.label: Plant Growth Regulators
nodes.description: Auxins, gibberellins, abscisic acid and ethene; phototropism and gravitropism; commercial applications of plant hormones

nodes.label: Meiosis
nodes.description: Meiosis I and II; reduction division; independent assortment; crossing over; chiasmata; significance of meiosis in producing genetic variation

nodes.label: Mendelian Genetics
nodes.description: Monohybrid and dihybrid crosses; dominance, codominance and multiple alleles; genotype and phenotype; Punnett squares; test crosses

nodes.label: Autosomal Linkage & Sex Linkage
nodes.description: Autosomal linkage; sex determination; sex-linked inheritance; examples including haemophilia and colour blindness; recombination frequency

nodes.label: Epistasis
nodes.description: Gene interactions; epistasis producing modified dihybrid ratios (9:3:4, 9:7, 12:3:1); examples of epistatic interactions

nodes.label: Chi-Squared Test
nodes.description: Chi-squared test for goodness of fit; null hypothesis; degrees of freedom; critical values; interpretation of results

nodes.label: Continuous & Discontinuous Variation
nodes.description: Polygenic inheritance; environmental influence on phenotype; continuous and discontinuous variation; normal distribution; nature vs nurture

nodes.label: Natural Selection
nodes.description: Variation, overproduction, competition, survival of the fittest; stabilising, directional and disruptive selection; antibiotic resistance as an example

nodes.label: Speciation
nodes.description: Allopatric and sympatric speciation; geographical and reproductive isolation; adaptive radiation; evidence for evolution

nodes.label: Hardy-Weinberg Principle
nodes.description: Hardy-Weinberg equations (p + q = 1, p2 + 2pq + q2 = 1); conditions for equilibrium; calculating allele and genotype frequencies

nodes.label: Artificial Selection
nodes.description: Selective breeding in plants and animals; inbreeding; outbreeding; comparison with natural selection; ethical considerations

nodes.label: Species Diversity
nodes.description: Species richness and evenness; Simpson's index of diversity; measuring biodiversity; importance of maintaining biodiversity

nodes.label: Classification & Taxonomy
nodes.description: Taxonomy: domain, kingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus, species; three-domain system; binomial nomenclature; use of DNA for classification

nodes.label: Conservation
nodes.description: Reasons for conservation; in situ and ex situ conservation; seed banks, captive breeding; protected areas; international agreements (CITES)

nodes.label: Ecosystems & Energy Flow
nodes.description: Ecosystems; food chains and food webs; trophic levels; energy transfer; pyramids of energy; productivity (gross and net)

nodes.label: Nutrient Cycling
nodes.description: Carbon cycle; nitrogen cycle; roles of decomposers, nitrifying bacteria, denitrifying bacteria, nitrogen-fixing bacteria; impact of human activities

nodes.label: Populations & Succession
nodes.description: Population growth curves; carrying capacity; limiting factors; ecological succession; climax communities; sampling methods

nodes.label: Tools of Genetic Technology
nodes.description: Restriction endonucleases; DNA ligase; vectors (plasmids, bacteriophages); reverse transcriptase; sticky and blunt ends

nodes.label: PCR & Gel Electrophoresis
nodes.description: Polymerase chain reaction: primers, Taq polymerase, thermal cycling; gel electrophoresis for separating DNA fragments; DNA profiling

nodes.label: Genetic Engineering
nodes.description: Recombinant DNA technology; transformation of host cells; production of human insulin and other proteins using GM bacteria; marker genes

nodes.label: Genetically Modified Organisms
nodes.description: GM crops (Bt corn, golden rice); GM animals; benefits and risks of GMOs; ethical, social and economic implications

nodes.label: Gene Therapy
nodes.description: Somatic and germ-line gene therapy; treatment of genetic diseases (SCID, cystic fibrosis); vectors for delivery; ethical considerations

nodes.label: Genome Projects & Bioinformatics
nodes.description: Human Genome Project; DNA sequencing methods; bioinformatics; comparative genomics; pharmacogenomics; proteomics

nodes.label: Gene Expression Regulation
nodes.description: Control of gene expression in prokaryotes (lac operon) and eukaryotes; transcription factors; epigenetics; methylation and histone modification

nodes.label: RNA Processing & Splicing
nodes.description: Post-transcriptional modification; introns and exons; alternative splicing; production of different proteins from one gene

nodes.label: Stem Cells & Cell Differentiation
nodes.description: Totipotent, pluripotent and multipotent stem cells; embryonic and adult stem cells; induced pluripotent stem cells; therapeutic uses and ethics

nodes.label: Gene Editing (CRISPR-Cas9)
nodes.description: CRISPR-Cas9 mechanism; guide RNA; applications in research and medicine; comparison with other gene editing tools; ethical implications
